on_this_day: 1545 — The first landing of French troops on the coast of the Isle of Wight during the French invasion of the Isle of Wight.

2001 — At the conclusion of a fireworks display on Okura Beach in Akashi, Hyōgo, Japan, 11 people are killed and more than 120 are injured when a pedestrian footbridge connecting the beach to JR Asagiri Station becomes overcrowded and people leaving the event fall down in a domino effect.
1718 — The Treaty of Passarowitz between the Ottoman Empire, Austria and the Republic of Venice is signed.
1645 — Qing dynasty regent Dorgon issues an edict ordering all Han Chinese men to shave their forehead and braid the rest of their hair into a queue identical to those of the Manchus.
1960 — Sirimavo Bandaranaike is elected Prime Minister of Sri Lanka, becoming the world's first female head of government
285 — Diocletian appoints Maximian as Caesar and co-ruler.
1775 — Battle of Brewster Isand : Patriot minutemen in whaleboats, commanded by Major Joseph Vose, raid Nantasket Point, also known as Little Brewster Island, in Boston Harbor, Massachusetts.
1656 — The Raid on Málaga takes place during the Anglo-Spanish War.
1873 — At Adair, Iowa, Jesse James and the James-Younger Gang pull off the first successful train robbery in the American Old West.
1242 — Battle of Taillebourg: Louis IX of France puts an end to the revolt of his vassals Henry III of England and Hugh X of Lusignan.
1568 — Eighty Years' War: Battle of Jemmingen: Fernando Álvarez de Toledo, Duke of Alva defeats Louis of Nassau.
1976 — Christopher Ewart-Biggs, the British ambassador to the Republic of Ireland, is assassinated by the Provisional IRA.
